body.title: Context7: MCP server for current docs at sprint time
body.result: context7 MCP server registered via claude mcp add; node v24.16.0 installed via nvm; context7 shows Connected in claude mcp list; requires new session to appear in deferred tools
body.intention: I intend that sprint sessions can fetch current version-specific documentation via Context7 MCP to address training cutoff staleness on fast-moving APIs.
body.description:
Context7 is an MCP server that fetches current, version-specific library/API docs at query time — solves the training data staleness problem when working with rapidly-changing APIs (Claude SDK, new models, etc.). Wire it into the MCP server list so it's available during sprints.

Reference: https://www.xda-developers.com/claude-code-good-mods-make-actually-production-ready/

**Affected files:** MCP config (wherever cc MCP servers are registered — discovery step)

**Design rules:** none apply

**Scope boundary:** In scope: install and register Context7 MCP server; verify it's queryable during a sprint. Out of scope: auto-injecting docs into every sprint (that's a follow-on), changing existing MCP tools.

**Completion criteria:** `context7` appears in the deferred tools list at session start; querying it for a known library (e.g. anthropic SDK) returns current docs.
